inherited_exposed_result: parent 90d base +166.78%, max drawdown -36.36%, failed >-35% gate; parent holdouts unopened
question: Can an own-positive-trend filter applied after bottom-3 realized-volatility selection preserve profitability while repairing the parent's excessive drawdown across untouched rule-level periods?
selection: bottom 3 realized volatility, retain each only when own same-window return is positive
development_gate: 90d base/stress >0; max drawdown >-30% and >=5pp better than unfiltered; 60d/180d base >0; turnover <=30
evaluation_gate: 90d base/stress >0; at least one positive year; max drawdown >-30% and below unfiltered; 60d/180d base >0
confirmation_gate: 90d base/stress >0; max drawdown >-25% and below unfiltered; 60d/180d base >=0; evaluation+confirmation compounded >0
holdout_release_rule: reuse exposed development after checkpoint; fetch evaluation only after pass; confirmation only after evaluation pass
